info.title: Contract API
info.description:
Generic REST API for interacting with Aptos Move contracts.

## Endpoints

- **POST /v1/execute** — Enqueue an entry function transaction (returns `202` with `queued`)
- **POST /v1/query** — Call a view function (sync)
- **GET /v1/transactions/{id}** — Poll transaction status

## Async Submission Model

Execute requests return `202 Accepted` with a `transaction_id` and `status: queued`. A background worker signs and submits to Aptos in FIFO order per sender. Use `GET /v1/transactions/{id}` to poll until `submitted` or `confirmed`.

### Transaction Lifecycle

1. **queued** — Persisted in MySQL, waiting for the submitter
2. **processing** — Claimed by a worker (build/sign/submit in progress)
3. **submitted** — Sent to the Aptos blockchain, awaiting confirmation
4. **confirmed** — Successfully committed on-chain
5. **failed** — Submission or confirmation failed (may be retried)
6. **expired** — Transaction expired before confirmation

## Authentication

All endpoints except `/v1/health` and `/v1/openapi.yaml` require an API key passed in the `Authorization` header. Requests without a valid key receive `401 Unauthorized`.

## Error Handling

Errors return a JSON body with a single `error` field:
```json
{"error": "description of the problem"}
```

## Arguments

Arguments are untyped (strings, numbers, arrays) — matching the native Aptos REST API convention. The server resolves argument types by fetching the module ABI from the Aptos node (cached per module) and handles BCS serialization automatically.

Supported Move types: `address`, `bool`, `u8`–`u256`, `0x1::string::String`, `vector<T>`, `0x1::object::Object<T>`.

## Address Format

All address parameters accept Aptos account addresses as hex strings (e.g., `0x1`). Addresses are validated on the server side; invalid addresses return `400 Bad Request`.

## Request Validation

- Request bodies must be valid JSON with `Content-Type: application/json`
- Unknown fields in the request body are rejected (`400 Bad Request`)
- Missing required fields return `400 Bad Request` with a descriptive message

tags.name: Execute
tags.description: Submit entry function transactions to any Move contract

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Call view functions on any Move contract

post.description:
Submits a transaction calling the specified Move entry function. Arguments are untyped — the server resolves types from the on-chain module ABI and handles BCS serialization. Returns 202 with a transaction_id for polling.

**Example: Mint tokens**
```json
{
  "function_id": "0x1234::contractInt::mint",
  "type_arguments": [],
  "arguments": ["0x5678abcd...", "10000"],
  "signer": "minter",
  "max_gas_amount": 200000
}
```

202.description: Transaction accepted and queued for submission

400.description: Invalid request (bad function_id format, wrong argument count, unsupported type, etc.)

500.description: Internal server error (ABI fetch failure, signing error, etc.)

post.description:
Calls a Move view function and returns the result synchronously. The request is proxied to the Aptos node's /view endpoint, which handles ABI resolution and argument serialization internally.

**Example: Check balance**
```json
{
  "function_id": "0x1234::contractInt::balance_of",
  "type_arguments": [],
  "arguments": ["0x5678abcd..."]
}
```

Returns: `{"result": ["10000"]}`

400.description: Invalid request or Aptos view function error (bad function_id, wrong args, on-chain abort, etc.)

502.description: Aptos node unreachable or returned an unexpected error

get.description: Serves an interactive API documentation page powered by Scalar, which reads from the OpenAPI spec at /v1/openapi.yaml.

200.description: HTML page with interactive API documentation

get.description: Returns the server health status. This endpoint does not require authentication.

Returns the current status and details of a previously submitted transaction. Use this endpoint to poll for transaction confirmation after calling the execute endpoint. Terminal statuses are: confirmed, failed, expired, permanently_failed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Transaction ID (UUID) returned by the execute endpoint

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response returned for all 4xx and 5xx status codes.

ExecuteRequest.description: Request body for executing a Move entry function as a blockchain transaction.

arguments.description: Function arguments as untyped values (strings, numbers, arrays). The server resolves the expected types from the on-chain module ABI and serializes via BCS.

items.description: Argument value — type is inferred from the on-chain ABI

function_id.description: Fully qualified Move function ID in the format addr::module::function

max_gas_amount.description: Optional gas limit override for this transaction. If omitted, the server's default max_gas_amount is used.

signer.description: Name of the configured signer role to sign the transaction (e.g., minter, owner, denylister)

type_arguments.description: Move type arguments for generic functions (e.g., ["0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin"]). Pass an empty array if the function has no type parameters.

HealthResponse.description: Health check response indicating server status.

QueryRequest.description: Request body for calling a Move view function (read-only, no transaction).

arguments.description: View function arguments as untyped values. The Aptos node resolves types from the on-chain ABI.

items.description: Argument value — type is inferred by the Aptos node

function_id.description: Fully qualified Move view function ID in the format addr::module::function

type_arguments.description: Move type arguments for generic view functions. Pass an empty array if the function has no type parameters.

QueryResponse.description: Response from a successful view function call.

result.description: View function return values as a JSON array. Element types depend on the Move function's return signature (e.g., ["10000"] for a u64 return).

items.description: Return value — type depends on the Move function signature

SubmitResponse.description: Response returned when a transaction is accepted for processing.

status.description: Initial transaction status after enqueue (always "queued" until the submitter runs)

transaction_id.description: Unique identifier for tracking the transaction via GET /v1/transactions/{id}

TransactionRecord.description: Full record of a submitted transaction, including its current lifecycle status.

attempt.description: Current attempt number (0-indexed). Incremented on each automatic retry.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the transaction was first accepted by the API

error_message.description: Error message from the most recent failure, if any

id.description: Unique transaction identifier assigned by the API

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts before the transaction is marked permanently_failed

sequence_number.description: Aptos account sequence number used when the transaction was built (set after submission)

request_payload.description: Original JSON request body stored for audit logging and transaction resubmission

sender_address.description: Aptos account address of the signer who submitted the transaction

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the transaction

txn_hash.description: On-chain transaction hash (populated after submission to the Aptos node)

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent status update

apiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
apiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Pass the raw key value in the Authorization header.
